titles.title: BOWOW Act of 2025
Bill to Outlaw Wounding of Official Working Animals Act
BOWOW Act
Bill to Outlaw Wounding of Official Working Animals Act of 2025
BOWOW Act of 2025
To amend the Immigration and Nationality Act to provide that an alien who has been convicted of harming animals used in law enforcement is inadmissible and deportable, and for other purposes.

action.text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
Committee Consideration and Mark-up Session Held
Committee Consideration and Mark-up Session Held
Ordered to be Reported (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 18 - 12.
Reported (Amended) by the Committee on Judiciary. H. Rept. 119-407.

calendar.text: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 355.

action.text: Rules Committee Resolution H. Res. 1115 Reported to House. Rule provides for consideration of H.R. 556, H.R. 1958 and H.R. 4638. The resolution provides for consideration of H.R. 556, H.R. 1958, and H.R. 4638 under a closed rule. The resolution provides for one hour of general debate and one motion to recommit on each bill.

committee.name: House Judiciary
committee.activity: Reported By, Markup By, Markup By, Referred To

term.name: Crimes against animals and natural resources

bill.summary:
Bill to Outlaw Wounding of Official Working Animals Act or the BOWOW Act
This bill establishes that a non-U.S. national (alien under federal law) convicted of, or who admits to having committed, an offense related to harming animals used in law enforcement is inadmissible and deportable.
